name: Domain Layer Purity Check
description: Tier B (agentStop): Verifies domain layer has no infrastructure dependencies. Fires after session — intermediate states may have temporary imports.

then.prompt:
## Package Territory Check (MANDATORY — evaluate FIRST)
Before performing any checks below, verify each file's path in your scan.

EXCLUDED ZONES (package infrastructure — NOT compliance subjects):
• .kiro/hooks/ — AI-GCE hook infrastructure
• .kiro/steering/ — AI-DWG/AI-GCE steering infrastructure
• .kiro/agents/ — AI-GCE agent infrastructure
• .governance/ — AI-GCE governance rules/agents/logs
• compliance-log/ — AI-GCE audit trail
• project-initiation/ — AI-PILC output
• architecture/ — AI-ADLC output
• management_framework/ — Shared governance spine
• docs/compliance-* — AI-GCE generated dashboards
• templates/ — AI-DWG planning templates

For each file in your scan: if its path starts with ANY of the above prefixes, SKIP it silently. Only check files outside excluded zones.

---

Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Construction phase onward.

Review all files modified during this session that are in domain layer paths (e.g., **/domain/**). For each:

1. NO INFRASTRUCTURE IMPORTS: Scan for using/import statements referencing infrastructure concerns (ORMs, HTTP clients, external packages beyond shared kernel).
2. NO PUBLIC SETTERS: Entities should use methods for state changes, not public property setters.
3. NO EXTERNAL SERVICE CALLS: Domain layer must not make HTTP calls or database queries directly.
4. SHARED KERNEL ONLY: Only references to shared kernel (Result<T>, Entity bases, Value Objects) are allowed.

If violations found, warn with DOM-005 and explain the correct DDD pattern.
If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "domain-layer-purity", "trigger": "agentStop", "ruleId": "DOM-005", "ruleSeverity": "critical", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}"}
Log ONE event per file checked.
